text:
If curious how much weight he demonstrated in this video, I noted them down, some are just guesses as I don't know exact brick weight, since most machines use 10 lbs per brick I assume the same here.

Day 1:
1. Flat DB press - 30kg each hand, 60kg total.
2. Incline DB row - 30kg each hand, 60kg total.
3. Chest Fly Machine - Level 8 on the bricks, probably 80 pounds / 36kg.
4. Reverse Fly / Reverse Pec Deck - Level 6 on the bricks, probably 60 pounds / 27kg.
5. DB Curls - 12kg each hand, 24kg total.

Day 2:
1. Leg Press Sled Machine - 105kg on each side, total 210kg. 
2. Seated Leg Curl - Level 8 on brick, probably 80 pounds / 36kg.
3. Leg Extension - Level 8 on brick, probably 80 pounds / 36kg.
4. Standing Calf Raise - Level 7 on brick, 70 pounds / 31.5kg.
5. Hyperextension - Bodyweight only.

Day 3:
1. Assisted Pullup.
2. Seated Smith Machine - 10kg each side, the smith bar probably 10kg, bringing total 30kg.
3. Cable Straight Arm Pulldown - Level 11 on brick, probably 110 pounds / 50kg.
4. Lateral Raise Machine - Level 7 on brick, 70 pounds / 31.5kg.
5. Rope Overhead Tricep Extension - Level 7 on brick, 70 pounds / 31.5kg.

You built that physique by going to the gym.

1. Push - Horizontal & Vertical
2. Pull - Horizontal & Vertical
3. Squat
4. Lunge
5. Hinge 
6. Carry
7. Locomotion

text:
Hi Josh, I heard about your channel from Jude (JumpRopeVeteran) and have been watching a lot of the videos; lots of useful information.
I have been doing body-weight exercises (since Aug 2023) and jump rope (using the Rhythm Rope since Nov 2023). In addition, I have been watching what I eat. I am ensuring that my routines and eating habits are sustainable. I have now lost around 18 lbs. 

Since I really liked this video, I have started doing this as a 5-set workout 3x a week. I have also incorporated hanging into my workout and rope flow during the dynamic rest (simple underhand/overhand swings). The other days is 30 min of jump rope and everyday has a 3+ mile walk. 

Thanx for these videos again; very helpful.
